73688.subject: рҹ”· The Daily: Robinhood says AI-powered crypto trading is coming
73688.body:
Mastercard secures New York BitLicense as it continues to expand crypto services | Singapore charges ex-Hodlnaut CEO over allegedly misleading claims tied to Terra collapse, faces up to 20 years in prison | Coinbase taps Standard Chartered to expand multi-currency funding rails for institutional clients | Spot Bitcoin ETFs continue outflow streak amid $1.3 billion IBIT whale trade |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Kyle Baird

Presented by

LMAX ( https://www.theblock.co/lmax?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Happy Wednesday! *Bitcoin's* 84-day streak of negative 30-day funding rates came to an end last week, according to *K33* Head of Research *Vetle Lunde* , narrowly missing the 2019 record but historically signaling the potential for a stronger recovery.

In today's newsletter, *Robinhood* says AI-powered crypto trading is coming soon, *Mastercard* secures a New York State *BitLicense* , *Singapore* charges the former *Hodlnaut* CEO over allegedly misleading claims tied to *Terra's* collapse, and more.

Meanwhile, pro-crypto *Christian Menefee* unseated ( https://www.theblock.co/post/402678/pro-crypto-menefee-unseats-al-green-fairshake?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) *Al Green* in a Texas Democratic primary runoff, supported by *Fairshake*

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read вҖ” and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*рҹӨ– Robinhood says AI-powered crypto trading coming after launching agentic stock trading beta*

( https://www.theblock.co/post/402729/robinhood-ai-powered-crypto-trading-launching-agentic-stock-trading-beta?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

*Robinhood* said it plans to let customers use AI agents to trade crypto on their behalf as part of a broader push into agentic finance.

* Robinhood launched the beta version of its agentic trading platform for equities on Wednesday, allowing users to connect AI agents to manage trading activity separately from their main portfolios.
* The company said support for crypto, options, futures, and event contracts is "coming soon," once the initiative expands beyond beta.
* Robinhood said customers can connect AI agents to its AI-native MCP servers to integrate trading tools directly into the platform without relying on unofficial APIs or workarounds.
* The news comes as crypto firms, including *Coinbase* and *Gemini* , increa

73686.subject: Crypto Got Cold Feet
73686.body:
----------
_**Your Weekly Deep Dive into The World of Crypto**_

**May 27, 2026 В· ****5 Min Read**

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/0bc20812-b70c-4080-83ec-e20bf130125a/image.png?t=1779867410)
Caption: Market SnapshotВ  В· As of May 27_В  |В  _BTC Price _В |В  _YTD Return _В |В  _Fear & Greed _В |В  _ETF Flows (Weekly)В _В _

## рҹ“Ў** THIS WEEK IN CRYPTO**

**GM, Readers!**

This was the week crypto got cold feet. Bitcoin spent seven days trapped in a $76K to $77.5K box, the Fear & Greed Index sat at 30, and the only conviction belonged to sellers. _The spark?_ The SEC quietly delayed its plan to let firms trade tokenized stocks, and roughly $320M in leveraged long bets vanished in about an hour.

Spot Bitcoin ETFs piled on, bleeding for six straight sessions. Ethereum had its own miserable week, losing a marquee backer and key researchers. The one bright spot was buried in Washington.В 

_**Let's get into itвҖҰ**_


----------View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/8132e416-70db-495d-8fec-7c68d66b46d5/image.png?t=1778654852)
Caption: 

----------
# рҹҗӢ THE BIG WHALE MOVES

### **1. ****The SEC Pulls a Rug on a Rug-Free Trade**

For weeks, traders had front-run a regulatory green light: the SEC's staff was reportedly close to approving an exemption letting US firms trade tokenized stocks such as Apple and Tesla. Leverage piled up.

On May 22, Bloomberg reported the plan was being delayed.

**The reaction was instant:** Around $320M in crypto longs were liquidated, and Bitcoin slid toward $76,000, its lowest in about a week.

**The lesson is old:** Positioning for a catalyst that has not happened is its own kind of gamble.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/8257cfb3-1ff4-41b4-a8e4-056705dfbe9d/image.png?t=1779867486)
Caption: _Chart: Bitcoin price over the past 7 days_

### **2. ****ETFs Head for the Exits**

US spot Bitcoin ETFs spent the week in full retreat, a sixth straight session of outflows. The five trading days from May 18 to May 22 saw roughly **$1.26B drained**, nearly matching the prior week's exodus.

It is a sharp turn from AprilвҖҷs strongest month for ETF demand. Net inflows for all of 2026 have now diminished to around $536M. The funds still hold roughly 727,000 BTC, so the long-term story is intact. But when BlackRock's IBIT has a bad week, the whole complex feels it.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/100d877e-a8a0-4684-bcaa-f5f35728db78/image.png?t=1779867527)
Caption: 

### **3. ****Ethereum Can't Catch a Break**

If Bitcoin's week was dull, Ethereum's was demoralising. Harvard Management Company's endowment liquidated its entire $87M ETH

73685.subject: Donald TrumpвҖҷs approval rating hits an all-time low
73685.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4ODl9AAcAAAAAAe_6Onc3BPnI3G4oq9wD5BO_2jptoxpE5drArd-KjfTLCMVTx32hpNpy9l6GG-Z_RlH0WKy_l8G6pwBg-TqMKktoyJPyxS2rMWQbD6tDXQIN9g 

May 27th 2026

 

 
 
 

 The Economist Today
 


 Our best journalism, handpicked each day
 


 
 
 

 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4ODl9AAcAAAAAAe_6Onc48_Gnhb6C13gzDdyYIq9W8X_hXHGl0Z4UjOtcyuY1E8qJyqwCWrvcfOH9HeaHP2sK87xJVzxEhWv7YjPQa6siba0w_qcW5n0jwujorA 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4ODl9AAcAAAAAAe_6Onc5zJ-WsXIqstYxMbuHTQHBQjRFo6hpbK5X-ZLATydVDVlvUPMiDffN20GMxH_x6e43PzW59X3jzaTdiDEDYQUDCxNuNae3I-Mt_Ppw8g 



 
 

Bo Franklin

Senior digital editor



Each week The Economist and YouGov ask Americans what they make of their president. The latest survey shows Donald Trump has passed a milestone: he is the most unpopular Oval Office occupant since our poll began in 2009. War in Iran has hurt Mr Trump’s standing, but it is his handling of the economy (which cannot be separated from his foreign policy) that is really dragging him down. Dig into the polling data in 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4ODl9AAcAAAAAAe_6Onc6itkyTFVZyrTV1-toTKMHomBeWzYgEhqwdPmJf_QvwHA4CPRY4VIKiC6dM4_37fwQ6pQJ3O3IuQ46JgJVIisDKR8TLlbCVIQt 
our Trump tracker .


And Ukraine has achieved something extraordinary simply by remaining sovereign. But survival alone is not victory. Our correspondent the

рҹ§  AIвҖҷs Crypto News Brief: TodayвҖҷs market mood is one of disciplined institutional expansion. While global payout rails and corporate treasuries reach new milestones, regulatory friction in Washington persists, and mysterious whale activity adds a touch of classic crypto uncertainty. рҹҢҗCircle and Nium Partner for Global Stablecoin Payouts in 190 Countries Circle and Nium have launched a partnership to connect USDC settlement with global payout rails across 190 countries and 100 currencies. The integration allows financial institutions to bypass traditional prefunding requirements, enabling real-time cross-border payments via the Circle Payments Network. Read full story рҹҸӣпёҸRipple National Trust Bank Charter Triggers Congressional Scrutiny RippleвҖҷs move to join federal banking infrastructure with a national trust charter is now live, aiming to hold RLUSD reserves directly at the Federal Reserve. However, Senator Elizabeth Warren has formally challenged the legality of the OCC's approval, sparking a high-stakes regulatory battle over "crypto banks." See details рҹӣ‘SEC Pauses "Innovation Exemption" for Third-Party Tokenized Stocks The SEC has halted a controversial plan that would have allowed crypto-native platforms to trade tokenized versions of public stocks without the issuerвҖҷs consent. The decision follows intense lobbying from traditional exchanges like Nasdaq and the NYSE, which cited risks of market fragmentation and investor protection gaps. Read full story рҹ’іCoinbase Taps Standard Chartered to Expand Institutional Fiat Rails Coinbase has integrated Standard CharteredвҖҷs banking infrastructure to offer multi-currency funding and settlement for institutional clients. The partnership adds support for AUD, SGD, CAD, and CHF, allowing global trading desks to manage capital efficiency without forced foreign exchange conversions through a single base currency. See details рҹ”ҘSatoshi-Era Bitcoin Whale Burns $8.3 Million in BTC Supply In a rare on-chain event, five dormant wallets dating back to 2014 simultaneously transferred 107 BTC to a well-known burn address. This permanent removal of $8.3 million from the circulating supply has sparked intense speculation among analysts regarding the motive behind this irreversible "exit" from the network. 73677.subject: The Slow Bleed
73677.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/eaa70932-7ce4-467e-b338-a662ed575d6a/0xR_NL_Banner_Heading_Wordmark.jpg?t=1776775276)
Caption: 

==Hi all, happy Wednesday! Today, we're diving into the crypto market's slow bleed, analyzing why compressed spot volume and ask-skewed order books point to a protracted distribution regime. We also break down the SECвҖҷs delayed innovation exemption for tokenized stocks and what the regulatory pause means for onchain equity markets.==

вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/42e942ec-f31d-4957-8722-a3ac7b121e1e/0xR_NL_Market_Update.png?t=1767631151) [Market Update]
Caption: 

Bitcoin is trading at ~$77K this morning, and the order book is quietly telling a story the price tape isn't. Trusted spot volume has compressed back to levels last seen in 2023, while passive sellers continue to dominate the top of book at every price point below $80K. The setup is a slow-bleed structure, not a capitulation one.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/60b48249-f11e-49d7-8808-c3015acc6561/image.png?t=1779878826)
Caption: 

BTC's 30-day average trusted spot volume has fallen to roughly $5B per day, down from a peak above $25B in late 2024 / early 2025 (a ~75% drawdown in activity). ETH has tracked the same path, compressing from ~$13B at peak to ~$3B today. Futures show the same shape on a larger absolute base: BTC notional has come in from a $90B+ peak to roughly $45-50B, with ETH retreating from its late-2025 spike near $100B back below $40B. SOL volume, both spot and futures, is making fresh local lows. Across the curve, this is the lowest aggregate engagement the majors have seen in this cycle outside the 2022-2023 bear.В 

Thin tape on its own is not a directional signal. What makes the current regime worth flagging is what's happening at the top of the book.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/b835007a-9bf2-4d85-8692-cd96d75858a6/image.png?t=1779878826)
Caption: 

Aggregating bid and ask depth within 50 bps of mid across major venues, the order book has run persistently ask-skewed since the start of the year. The deepest dips (readings of -10% to nearly -20%), where ask-side depth dwarfs bids, have clustered in the windows where BTC is around $80K. Bid-side spikes still occur, but they are shorter-lived and shallower than the ask-side accumulation. At every fade rally toward the $80K handle, market makers are restocking offers faster than dip buyers are restocking bids.

This is what a distribution regime tends to look like in the data before it shows up in price. Spot volume is too thin to absorb a determined seller with
